title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Trump Endorses Congressman Who’s Been Missing For Months
stories.summary:
President Donald Trump has endorsed Republican Rep. Tom Kean Jr. for re-election in New Jersey’s 7th Congressional District, despite the lawmaker having missed more than 100 votes and remaining unseen in public for several months.

Kean secured his party's nomination without opposition on Tuesday as he seeks a third term in office.

The Democratic primary resulted in former Navy helicopter pilot Rebecca Bennett winning the nomination to challenge Kean in November’s general election.

Media outlets have characterized Kean’s prolonged absence from Washington and his district as mysterious, with reports citing an unspecified illness as the reason for his unavailability.

links.headline: Rebecca Bennett wins New Jersey Democratic primary, to face Trump ally Kean
links.excerpt: Democrats pick former Navy commander to challenge Republican Tom Kean in New Jersey's 7th District.
links.imageAlt: Rebecca Bennett wins New Jersey Democratic primary, to face Trump ally Kean

stories.title: Trump Taps Familiar Name To Replace Tulsi Gabbard
stories.summary:
President Donald Trump has appointed William J. "Bill" Pulte, the current Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, to serve as acting Director of National Intelligence.

This appointment follows the resignation of Tulsi Gabbard from the position last month. Pulte is described as a staunch ally of President Trump who has built a reputation for conducting aggressive institutional cleanups within federal agencies.

In this new role, Pulte will maintain his current responsibilities leading the Federal Housing Finance Agency while simultaneously overseeing national intelligence operations. His selection highlights a continued reliance on loyalists familiar with the administration's priorities.

Reports indicate that Pulte previously advocated for criminal charges against political opponents of the President, signaling a potentially hardline approach to his new duties as the nation's top spymaster.

links.headline: Trump taps housing official Bill Pulte to be US spymaster
links.excerpt: The head of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, who pushed for criminal charges against Trump's foes, will step in as acting Director of National Intelligence.
links.imageAlt: Trump taps housing official Bill Pulte to be US spymaster
links.alignmentBasis: source-default-fallback: no article alignment cues; source prior=Center
links.reliabilityBasis: source-prior=high; article-text: no reliability modifier cues

stories.title: Steyer Appears to Tear Up While Lavishing Praise on Man Who Won Girl's Track Event
stories.summary:
Reality television personality Spencer Pratt is running as a Republican candidate for mayor of Los Angeles, positioning himself in a competitive race against two Democratic opponents. Known primarily for his appearance on the MTV series The Hills, Pratt has sought to translate his media fame into political capital by campaigning on an anti-establishment platform.

His candidacy has drawn attention from prominent conservative figures, including comedian Bill Maher, who publicly endorsed Pratt on social media. Maher praised the candidate's perceived authenticity, stating that he was won over immediately upon hearing Pratt’s message.

This endorsement highlights the unusual intersection of entertainment and local politics in one of the nation's largest cities.

links.headline: He was a reality show villain. Can he be the mayor of one of America's largest cities?
links.excerpt: Republican Spencer Pratt, known for his TV role on The Hills, appears to be in a tight race with two Democrats in Los Angeles.
links.imageAlt: He was a reality show villain. Can he be the mayor of one of America's largest cities?

stories.title: European Union observers reject Petro's fraud claims, calling Colombia's vote 'transparent'
stories.summary:
The European Union's Electoral Observation Mission to Colombia has officially dismissed President Gustavo Petro's allegations of fraud following Sunday's presidential election. Observers characterized the vote count as transparent, orderly, and fluid, directly contradicting the sitting president's claims.

In a public statement released on Tuesday, the mission described the first round of voting as a lesson in democracy for the nation. This assessment stands in stark contrast to the bitter contest that unfolded during the election process.

links.headline: European election monitor dismisses rumours of fraud in Colombia’s election
links.excerpt: The electoral mission described the first round of voting in Colombia's presidential race as 'a lesson in democracy'.
links.imageAlt: European election monitor dismisses rumours of fraud in Colombia’s election

stories.title: Trump to attend delayed White House Correspondents’ Dinner after shooting
stories.summary:
President Donald Trump is set to attend the White House Correspondents’ Dinner, which has been rescheduled following a security incident earlier this year. The event was originally held in April when a gunman breached a security checkpoint, disrupting the proceedings and prompting an immediate postponement.

The dinner’s return comes amid heightened scrutiny regarding press freedom and media relations. Advocacy groups have expressed concern that the platform could be used by Trump to launch attacks against the news media, raising questions about the appropriateness of the event under current political tensions.

links.headline: Trump to attend delayed White House Correspondents’ Dinner after shooting
links.excerpt: Press freedom advocates, however, have warned that the dinner could give Trump a podium to attack the news media.

stories.title: Police respond to hostage situation and bomb threat at California bank
stories.summary:
Police and emergency responders converged on a downtown Bakersfield, California, bank shortly after 1 p.m. Tuesday following reports of a hostage situation and bomb threat.

Authorities reported that a man allegedly barricaded himself inside a JPMorgan Chase branch with at least one person while threatening to detonate explosives. The incident prompted immediate evacuations, street closures, and lockdowns at nearby government buildings as negotiators worked to secure the release of hostages.

stories.title: Supreme Court Hands Alabama Republicans Major Redistricting Victory
stories.summary:
The Supreme Court issued a 6-3 emergency order on Tuesday allowing Alabama to implement a new congressional redistricting map ahead of the 2026 midterm elections. This decision grants a significant victory to state Republicans by permitting the elimination of one of the two majority-minority districts currently in place.

The ruling lifts an injunction previously issued by a lower court, which had determined that the revised map intentionally discriminated against Black voters. By blocking that lower court's finding, the high court enables Alabama to proceed with the GOP-friendly boundaries for the upcoming election cycle.

Media outlets report that the new configuration could potentially help Republicans flip a Democratic-held seat in 2026. The decision concludes a prolonged legal battle over how congressional districts are drawn within the state.

links.headline: Supreme Court Hands Alabama Republicans Major Redistricting Victory
links.excerpt: The Supreme Court on Tuesday handed Alabama Republicans a significant victory in the state’s ongoing battle over congressional redistricting, allowing Alabama to use a map that eliminates one of its two majority-minority congressional districts ahead of the 2026 midterm elections. In a 6-3 emergency order, the Court granted Alabama’s request to implement the map adopted ...

stories.title: There’s nothing like the Isle of Man TT
stories.summary:
The Spectator Australia has published an article titled 'Canada is misremembering the Komagata Maru incident,' criticizing recent Canadian government actions regarding historical memory. The piece characterizes these developments as acts of national self-abasement, specifically referencing events surrounding May 23 and the Komagata Maru incident.

The publication argues that Canada's current approach to this historical event reflects a distorted or inaccurate recollection of the past. By framing recent governmental responses as excessive or misguided, the article suggests a broader critique of how national history is being interpreted and commemorated in contemporary Canadian discourse.

links.headline: Canada is misremembering the Komagata Maru incident

stories.title: Paedophile magistrate's release prompts vigilante warning
stories.summary:
South Australia Police have issued a stern warning regarding the upcoming release of Peter Liddy, a former magistrate convicted as a paedophile. Police Commissioner Grant Stevens confirmed that the force is prepared to take necessary actions to address public safety concerns once Liddy leaves prison.

Despite acknowledging these security measures, officials emphasized there would be zero tolerance for any vigilante activity directed at the released offender. The statement aims to balance community anxiety with strict adherence to legal boundaries and law enforcement protocols.

links.headline: Paedophile magistrate's release prompts vigilante warning
links.excerpt: South Australia's police chief says the force will respond accordingly to concerns about public safety once paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy is released from prison, but added there would be no tolerance for "vigilante activity".
links.imageAlt: Paedophile magistrate's release prompts vigilante warning

links.reliabilityBasis: source-prior=high; article-text: no reliability modifier cues

stories.imageAlt: Paedophile magistrate's release prompts vigilante warning

stories.title: Trucking in a fuel crisis: the Australian drivers sacrificing their paycheck for diesel – video
stories.summary:
Australian truck owner-drivers are facing severe financial pressure as rising diesel costs erode their earnings. Frank, an independent contractor who operates across the country for up to two months at a time, relies on a traditional budgeting model where one-third of job pay covers fuel, another third handles maintenance, and the final third constitutes his wages.

This structure is currently under strain due to increased diesel prices linked to the war in Iran. The surge in fuel costs threatens the viability of this payment split for owner-operators who must cover their own expenses.

Coupled with historically slim margins, drivers are being forced to sacrifice a portion of their paycheck just to keep moving.

links.headline: Trucking in a fuel crisis: the Australian drivers sacrificing their paycheck for diesel – video
links.excerpt: Frank is a truck owner-driver, meaning he owns his own truck, picks up contracts wherever he happens to be in Australia and pays for his own fuel. He can spend up to two months at a time on the road, and lives by the rule-of-thumb that one third of a job's pay should go to fuel, one third should go to truck maintenance and the remaining third is his wages. But the war in Iran, leading to increased diesel prices, has put significant financial strain on this model. Coupled with historically sli
links.imageAlt: Trucking in a fuel crisis: the Australian drivers sacrificing their paycheck for diesel – video

links.reliabilityBasis: source-prior=high; article-text: no reliability modifier cues

stories.title: Trump signs executive order seeking early access to new AI releases
stories.summary:
President Donald Trump signed an executive order establishing a voluntary framework for the federal government to review powerful new artificial intelligence models before their public release. The directive represents a significant shift from earlier proposals that would have mandated mandatory safety reviews, reflecting substantial pushback from technology industry stakeholders.

Reports indicate that the final version of the order was narrowed significantly after objections from major tech companies concerned about regulatory overreach and competitive disadvantages. This reversal has drawn criticism from some observers who argue it grants unchecked power to big tech by removing compulsory safety checkpoints for advanced AI systems.

The executive order aims to balance national security concerns with industry innovation, allowing the government access to proprietary models for vetting purposes without imposing strict compliance requirements. Tech firms are now asked to share their most advanced developments voluntarily, marking a departure from more stringent oversight measures initially discussed by the administration.

stories.title: Call of controversy? Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 imagines a revived Korean war
stories.summary:
Infinity Ward’s upcoming Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 is set to feature a revived Korean War, marking another potentially controversial real-world setting for the franchise. The game continues a tradition established by earlier entries in the series that often sparked public debate over their depiction of conflict.

Previous installments have drawn significant attention for sensitive missions. In 2009’s Modern Warfare 2, players encountered the infamous “No Russian” level involving civilian casualties in a Moscow airport.

More recently, 2022’s entry included a drone strike mission that mirrored the real-world US assassination of Iranian general Qassem Suleimani.

links.headline: Call of controversy? Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 imagines a revived Korean war
links.excerpt: Infinity Ward’s new game in the storied shooter genre embraces change with a potentially controversial real-world setting There was a time when Call of Duty (CoD) regularly courted controversy. In 2009, Modern Warfare 2’s infamous “No Russian” mission saw players (optionally) shooting screaming civilians in a Moscow airport. In 2022’s entry, a drone strike mission that drew chilling parallels to the real-world US assassination of Iranian general Qassem Suleimani two years earlier was fe
links.imageAlt: Call of controversy? Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 imagines a revived Korean war

stories.title: AI ‘art’ is boring, soulless theft – and when I see it as an artist I see red | Jess Harwood
stories.summary:
Visual artist Jess Harwood argues that AI-generated art represents soulless theft and diminishes human creativity. Writing in The Guardian, she contrasts her traditional hand-drawn methods with the perceived emptiness of algorithmic output.

Harwood reflects on attending a Split Enz concert alone, where she experienced genuine joy and heartache from lyrics rooted in lived human experience. She notes that this emotional connection eliminates any need to question whether the music was AI-assisted.

The piece suggests that relying on artificial intelligence would strip color and authenticity from artistic existence rather than enhancing it.

links.headline: AI ‘art’ is boring, soulless theft – and when I see it as an artist I see red
links.excerpt: I draw the old way – with my hand. Doing it with AI would not make me more creative, it would drain the colour out of my existence Last week I went to a gig by myself for the first time. I sat myself down in my single seat, possibly the youngest person in the room and one of thousands excited to see Split Enz. I loved it – I felt joy and heartache as the lyrics spoke of human experiences, really lived. I happily realised that I did not have to wonder whether Split Enz had used AI in the
links.imageAlt: AI ‘art’ is boring, soulless theft – and when I see it as an artist I see red | Jess Harwood
links.alignmentBasis: source-default-fallback: no article alignment cues; source prior=Lean Left
links.reliabilityBasis: source-prior=high; article-text: no reliability modifier cues

stories.title: Charities decry UK plan to use AI to assess age of young asylum seekers
stories.summary:
A coalition of more than 100 refugee children’s organizations has strongly criticized the UK Home Office's plan to implement artificial intelligence for assessing the age of young asylum seekers. The group warns that relying on facial age estimation technology could result in minors being incorrectly classified as adults, potentially leading to their placement in adult detention centers or prisons.

The announcement regarding the contract rollout came from the Home Office on Friday, sparking immediate backlash from advocacy groups concerned about the accuracy and ethical implications of such biometric assessments. Critics argue that the technology lacks sufficient reliability for making determinations that significantly impact vulnerable individuals' legal status and living conditions.

links.excerpt: Coalition of more than 100 organisations says move could lead to more children ending up in adult detention facilities A coalition of more than a hundred refugee children’s organisations has said controversial plans to use AI to assess the age of young asylum seekers could lead to more children wrongly ending up in adult prisons or detention centres. The warning follows a Home Office announcement on Friday of a contract to roll out AI facial age estimation technology on young asyl
links.imageAlt: Charities decry UK plan to use AI to assess age of young asylum seekers

stories.title: Meta legal action forces Facebook whistleblower to sit in silence at Hay festival
stories.summary:
Sarah Wynn-Williams, a former Facebook employee and author of the memoir Careless People, was prevented from speaking at the Hay festival due to ongoing legal action by Meta. Lawyers advised her against participating in a scheduled conversation with investigative journalist Carole Cadwalladr, citing potential sanctions from the tech giant.

The incident highlights the restrictive measures employed by Meta against former staff who have publicly detailed their experiences within the company. Wynn-Williams' silence on stage underscores the legal pressures faced by whistleblowers attempting to share their accounts in public forums.

links.headline: Meta legal action forces Facebook whistleblower to sit in silence at Hay festival
links.excerpt: Sarah Wynn-Williams did not speak during event after lawyers warned of possible sanctions from tech firm Facebook whistleblower Sarah Wynn-Williams was forced to sit in silence on stage at an event at Hay festival, after lawyers advised her not to speak because of ongoing legal action brought by Meta. Wynn-Williams, whose bestselling memoir, Careless People, details her years working at Facebook, was due to appear in conversation with the investigative journalist Carole Cadwalladr

stories.title: Complaint filed vs Cayetano, Padilla, Aplasca over Dela Rosa ‘escape’
stories.summary:
A coalition of political parties and civil society organizations has filed a complaint with the Office of the Ombudsman against Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano, Senator Robin Padilla, and suspended Senate Sergeant-at-Arms Mao Aplasca. The legal action addresses the circumstances surrounding Senator Ronald "Bato" Dela Rosa's departure from Senate premises last month.

The complaint specifically cites obstruction of justice in relation to Dela Rosa's exit. Reports indicate that Sgt.-at-Arms Aplasca is included in the filing due to his alleged role in triggering the prior Senate shooting incident, which preceded the senator's departure.

links.headline: Complaint filed vs Cayetano, Padilla, Aplasca over Dela Rosa ‘escape’
links.excerpt: MANILA, Philippines — A coalition of political parties and civil society organizations on Wednesday filed a complaint before the Office of the Ombudsman against Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano, Sen. Robin Padilla, and suspended Senate sergeant-at-arms (SAA) Ma O. Aplasca in connection with Sen. Ronald “Bato” Dela Rosa’s departure from the Senate premises last month.

stories.title: Mayor Zamora: Let Estrada answer the charges and prove his innocence
stories.summary:
San Juan City Mayor Francis Zamora has urged the public to respect due process as Senator Jinggoy Estrada faces charges related to flood control projects. Zamora emphasized that legal proceedings should be allowed to take their course through the Sandiganbayan.

The Sandiganbayan’s Second Division postponed the arraignment of both Estrada and former Public Works Secretary Manuel Bonoan until June 4. Bonoan, who arrived in a wheelchair, experienced signs of hypertension, leading to the delay after he was hospitalized on Monday night.

links.headline: Arraignment of Estrada, Bonoan postponed
links.excerpt: MANILA, Philippines — The Sandiganbayan’s Second Division on Tuesday postponed the arraignment of Sen. Jinggoy Estrada and former Public Works Secretary Manuel Bonoan to June 4. In the case of Bonoan, who arrived in a wheelchair, his arraignment was postponed after he again showed signs of hypertension. On Monday night, he was taken to the

stories.title: BFAR faces scrutiny amid record fishing intrusions
stories.summary:
Lawmakers and conservation groups are demanding transparency from the Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources (BFAR) regarding commercial fishing activities in restricted zones. The pressure follows new satellite data revealing significant intrusions into municipal waters designated for small-scale fishers.

According to recent reports, satellite-based monitoring systems detected 3,853 instances of apparent commercial fishing vessels operating within these protected areas. These findings have intensified calls for the agency to release detailed vessel tracking information to verify the extent of unauthorized operations.

The scrutiny highlights ongoing tensions between industrial fishing interests and local communities reliant on municipal waters for their livelihoods. Advocates argue that releasing this data is crucial for enforcing existing regulations and protecting sustainable fisheries management.

links.headline: BFAR faces scrutiny amid record fishing intrusions
links.excerpt: MANILA, Philippines—Lawmakers and conservation groups are pressing the Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources (BFAR) to release vessel monitoring data from commercial fishing boats following fresh reports of continued fishing activity inside municipal waters reserved for small-scale fishers. The renewed calls come after satellite-based monitoring recorded 3,853 apparent commercial fishing vessel detections inside municipal waters
links.imageAlt: BFAR faces scrutiny amid record fishing intrusions

stories.title: Iloilo City returns to 5-day workweek on June 8
stories.summary:
Iloilo City will resume a standard five-day workweek for city hall operations beginning June 8. This decision follows a recommendation from the local crisis management council, which reviewed recent data and assessed the current situation.

The council determined that previously implemented austerity measures have produced positive results. These temporary adjustments successfully reduced the city's operational footprint while maintaining essential services.

links.headline: Iloilo City returns to 5-day workweek on June 8
links.excerpt: ILOILO CITY – Following a recommendation by the crisis management council, Iloilo City Hall operations will return to a regular five-day workweek starting on June 8. The council’s recommendation came after reviewing current data and assessing the situation, which indicated that the austerity measures implemented earlier have yielded positive results. These measures successfully reduced the city’s

stories.title: Sara Duterte sidesteps allegations in reply to impeachment case
stories.summary:
The House team of prosecutors in the Philippines expressed disappointment with Vice President Sara Duterte’s response to her impeachment case, stating that she relied on technicalities rather than addressing the core allegations directly. Prosecutors had anticipated a substantive reply regarding accusations involving confidential funds, bribery, unexplained wealth, and threats.

Duterte’s legal team submitted their answer to the articles of impeachment on Tuesday, but prosecutors argue that the response failed to provide clear explanations for the serious charges leveled against her. The House prosecutors emphasized that they expected a direct confrontation with the allegations rather than procedural defenses.

links.headline: Sara Duterte sidesteps allegations in reply to impeachment case
links.excerpt: MANILA, Philippines — The House team of prosecutors on Tuesday expressed disappointment with Vice President Sara Duterte’s answer to the articles of impeachment, assailing her for relying on technicalities instead of confronting the allegations head-on. “We had anticipated that we’ll finally see her answers on the confidential fund, bribery, unexplained wealth and even threats [allegations],
links.imageAlt: Sara Duterte sidesteps allegations in reply to impeachment case

stories.title: U.N. and Congolese Troops Struggle to Halt Lightning Rebel Attack
stories.summary:
United Nations and Congolese forces are struggling to contain a rapid offensive by the M23 rebel group in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo. The Rwandan-backed militia has seized two strategic towns on the outskirts of Goma in recent days, marking a significant escalation in the regional conflict.

Aid organizations estimate that approximately 300,000 civilians have been displaced as they flee the advancing rebel forces. The rapid territorial gains by M23 have raised urgent concerns about humanitarian conditions and security for residents in the affected areas.

links.headline: U.N. and Congolese Troops Struggle to Halt Lightning Rebel Attack
links.excerpt: Aid workers estimate as many as 300,000 people are fleeing advances by the Rwandan-backed M23 rebel group, which has taken two strategic towns on the approaches to Goma over the past several days.
links.imageAlt: U.N. and Congolese Troops Struggle to Halt Lightning Rebel Attack

stories.title: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
stories.summary:
OpenAI has introduced a new suite of specialized tools designed to assist with white-collar professional tasks. The company released six distinct plugins, each tailored to approximate the workflows of specific job roles.

These targeted applications cover areas such as data analytics, creative production, sales, product design, equity investing, and investment banking. Each plugin is accessible directly through the Codex application interface.

The tools are engineered to bundle necessary integrations, instructions, and contextual data. This structure allows the AI system to better simulate the functions of a dedicated professional in each respective field.

links.headline: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
links.excerpt: OpenAI released a set of six plug-ins aimed at specific jobs: data analytics, creative production, sales, product design, equity investing, and investment banking. Available from within the Codex app, each of the new tools bundles integrations, instructions, and context to allow Codex to approximate a specific job.
links.imageAlt: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work

stories.title: Why I’m grateful to the Pope for his encyclical on AI | Francine Prose
stories.summary:
Francine Prose expresses gratitude for the Pope’s recent encyclical on artificial intelligence, describing it as an intelligent and thoughtful warning regarding the uses and misuses of rapidly developing technology. She argues that Silicon Valley is wrong to dismiss such ethical guidance.

Prose addresses the common provocation she receives about whether future novels will be written by AI machines. While she often responds with a dismissal that algorithms cannot replicate masterpieces like Anna Karenina, she acknowledges this is not a substantive answer to her underlying concerns.

links.headline: Why I’m grateful to the Pope for his encyclical on AI
links.excerpt: The intelligent and thoughtful encyclical is an important warning of the uses and misuses of a rapidly developing technology. Silicon Valley is wrong to dismiss it Often I’m asked if I think that the novels of the future will all be written by AI. It’s not so much a question as a provocation. Do I worry that a machine can do what I do, only better? I usually say something like: “No algorithm is going to write Anna Karenina!” which is also not a real answer. So I’m grateful to Pope
links.imageAlt: Why I’m grateful to the Pope for his encyclical on AI | Francine Prose
links.alignmentBasis: source-default-fallback: no article alignment cues; source prior=Lean Left
links.reliabilityBasis: source-prior=high; article-text: no reliability modifier cues

stories.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
stories.summary:
President Donald Trump has proposed a plan to relocate Palestinians from Gaza, describing the war-torn enclave as a "demolition site" that needs to be "cleaned out." The suggestion involves moving refugees either temporarily or on a long-term basis to neighboring countries, specifically Egypt and Jordan.

This proposal aligns with rhetoric Trump has employed previously regarding the region. However, it faces immediate and firm rejection from Arab nations, which have opposed such displacement efforts since the onset of the war.

links.headline: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
links.excerpt: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.
links.imageAlt: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan

links.excerpt: The dictator, eager to shut out the influence of Hollywood and K-pop, has been exalting a “shock brigade” of 300,000 teens and 20-somethings drafted into flood reconstruction.
